titleStmt.title: The Secretary of State to the Ambassador in the United Kingdom ( (Winant))

div.head: The Secretary of State to the Ambassador in the United Kingdom ( Winant )
div.p: 10093. Question referred to in your despatch no. 18981Dated November 2, p. 1391. considered by this Department and the War Department to be one having important implications. Your 10541, November 29, 7 p.m.Not printed; it reported that the vote referred to in Department’s telegram 9920, November 25, to London, had been deferred to December 6 (740.00116 EW/11–2944). It may be impossible to send Pell instructions before December 6. We would prefer to have vote on matter postponed by Commission beyond December 6 for substantial length of time if possible.At the 41st meeting of the Commission, December 6, 1944, it was decided that “the subject would be adjourned until the members were ready to take it up again.” (740.00116 European War/12–1144) Matter is being actively considered by interested Departments here in connection with other related questions. Please repeat to Pell.
